reviews.review_description: The Experience crashes during feet alignment
I absolutely loved this so cool and free would buy it even wishing and hopeing for mor to be added! Beautiful very engagingand so different from any other narrative/ game in VR! Great job devs👍🏼🥰
It worked once and then hasn’t worked for about two weeks

Lavrynthos is an amazing game! Seriously, I had a blast playing it. While it’s a bit short—about 30 minutes to complete—it left a great impression on me. It’s a labyrinth-style game, similar to others I’ve played, but the design and execution here are top-notch. I also loved that you occasionally need to crawl, which adds an extra layer of immersion. The story is pretty engaging too, which makes the experience even more enjoyable. Overall, despite its brevity, it's definitely worth a play!

I love story-telling and impossible spaces and this gem combines both in a truely magnificent way. Following along the story by walking through the labyrinth yourself, seeing it from varying and surprising perspectives makes the time fly really fast in this short but impactful experience. There are no interactions but the transitions between the zones and the whole atmosphere are really well crafted. I can only recommend to download it.
Such an unexpected and heart-touching experience, congratulations!

reviews.review_title: Is funny story and walking maze sim, but
reviews.review_description: Even I have big enough room space, but you can't change the location where to begin, it auto set the worst position in my living room have a bar table I can't move, although I still can use my joystick to move around (you need 2x2m space, and there are no rotation for your body), but this make me much less immersive! if fix it I will give five stars.

Lavrynthos is a wonderful passive narrative experience. The set/character designs are great and the voice acting is very well done. Everything just works so well that you may not even realize it's also demonstrating one of VR's more interesting locomotion technique called redirected walking. It's one of my go to experiences for introducing non games to VR.
Incredible special effects, wonderful characters. Recommend 100%
This is the kind of experience I can never get enough of. Sweet, funny, and meta moments are sprinkled throughout the story. I highly recommend Labyrinth!

Lavrynthos brings us a beautiful story, unfold as we try to make our way out of an impressive maze.  It has become one of my favorite VR experiences ever! The story is so fun and cute and yet deep and meaningful. If your play area is bigger than 2x2 you won't need artificial locomotion, it is soo cool to be able to wander through a maze!
 I would also recommend you to use your body, crouch and  walk through the maze, if you can.
Allow yourself to get lost,  it will be a unique experience!
